description:
You are a platform engineer reviewing commit activity across several micro-service
    repositories that feed a company-wide CI/CD pipeline.  
    Three raw author logs have already been exported from the build server and placed
    for you on the filesystem.

    Existing data (do NOT modify these files):
      • /home/user/ci_data/commit_authors/service-a.log  
      • /home/user/ci_data/commit_authors/service-b.log  
      • /home/user/ci_data/commit_authors/service-c.log  

    Each line in every *.log file contains **exactly one e-mail address** (the author of a
    single commit).  There are no blank lines.

    Your tasks:

    1. Aggregate every *.log file that lives in  
       /home/user/ci_data/commit_authors/  (future pipelines will blindly drop new
       files here, so use a pattern rather than hard-coding file names).

    2. Produce a frequency report that counts how many commits each author made,
       then sort that report in **descending numeric order** by the commit count
       (highest commits first).

    3. Save the final report to  
       /home/user/ci_reports/commit_author_frequency.tsv  
       Create the /home/user/ci_reports directory if it does not already exist.

    4. Output format of commit_author_frequency.tsv  
       • The file must be UTF-8 encoded text.  
       • One author per line.  
       • Field 1: the total commit count (an integer).  
       • Field 2: the author’s e-mail address.  
       • The two fields must be separated by a **single TAB character (0x09)**.  
       • No extra leading/trailing white-space on any line.  
       • Lines must be terminated with a single Unix newline “\n”.  
       • Example of exactly how lines must look (⇥ represents a TAB; do NOT include
         the “⇥” symbol in your file):  
         15⇥jane.doe@example.com

    5. After generating the file, print to the console the line  
         Top author: <EMAIL> with <COUNT> commits  
       where <EMAIL> and <COUNT> are taken from the first line of your report.
       This console output is for human readability; the automated tests will only
       verify the contents of commit_author_frequency.tsv.

    Constraints / hints:
      • You must solve the counting problem using standard GNU command-line
        utilities such as sort, uniq, awk, cut, etc.  In production we do this
        completely in-stream without temporary scripting languages.  
      • Treat the *.log files as read-only.  Create new files only under your
        home directory tree.  
      • The test harness will inspect /home/user/ci_reports/commit_author_frequency.tsv
        for exact byte-level matches against the specification above, so mind
        the TAB and newline requirements.

    When you are done you should have:
      • /home/user/ci_reports/commit_author_frequency.tsv containing the properly
        sorted frequency table.
      • A console message identifying the top author.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth information that the automated grader will rely on.
    The agent must NOT see anything in this section.
  -->
  <existing_files>
    <file path="/home/user/ci_data/commit_authors/service-a.log"><![CDATA[
jane.doe@example.com
john.smith@example.com
jane.doe@example.com

john.smith@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
john.smith@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/ci_data/commit_authors/service-b.log"><![CDATA[
john.smith@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
john.smith@example.com
john.smith@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
john.smith@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/ci_data/commit_authors/service-c.log"><![CDATA[
john.smith@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
alex.lee@example.com
john.smith@example.com
john.smith@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
john.smith@example.com
alex.lee@example.com
john.smith@example.com
jane.doe@example.com
]]></file>
  </existing_files>

  <expected_output>
    <file path="/home/user/ci_reports/commit_author_frequency.tsv" must_exist="yes"><![CDATA[
15	jane.doe@example.com
12	john.smith@example.com
3	alex.lee@example.com
]]></file>
  </expected_output>
